title: Slice 6 leg-A headless oracle — link/table/image/bookmark survive clone exportDocx
leg: A (headless, no real Word needed — Tasks 7+11 autonomous part)
method: Temporary electron probe (scripts/_tmp-slice6-legA-probe.js, removed before commit). On a fresh WC.PM.newBlank() doc: insertLink({href:'https://example.com', text:'example'}) over a selected word, insertBookmark({name:'spot1'}) over a selected word, insertImage({src:<1x1 PNG data-url>}), insertTable({rows:3, cols:4}). Exported bytes via WC.PM.exportDocxBytes() and wrote them to /tmp/slice6-legA.docx via the bytes-IPC (window.wordAPI.saveBytes). Then inspected with `node scripts/docx-inspect.js /tmp/slice6-legA.docx`.

assertions.name: hyperlink relationship round-trips
assertions.expect: hyperlinks has >=1 rId, and relTargets includes Target 'https://example.com' for that rId

assertions.name: image embedded in word/media
assertions.expect: blips has >=1 rEmbed rId, and media has >=1 file with bytes>0

assertions.name: bookmark is a PAIRED start+end (Critique B1)
assertions.expect: bookmarks has >=1 entry with the name used ('spot1'), and bookmarkEnds contains a matching id
assertions.actual: bookmarks=[{id:'0', name:'spot1'}]; bookmarkEnds=['0'] (matching id 0 -> paired start+end)

overall_verdict: PASS — clone exportDocx produces valid OOXML; hyperlink rel, 3x4 table, embedded image (word/media, 70 bytes), and paired bookmark all survive the round-trip on a clone-authored .docx.
notes: Headless leg only. Word-dependent legs (reopen-in-Word fidelity, Word-authored leg B) deferred to a later session with Word windowed. No gate was changed and real Word was not touched.
